from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into one fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    subject = triples[0].subject

    # Clean object values to avoid redundant units
    def clean_object(obj, predicate):
        obj = str(obj)
        if predicate in ["height", "floorArea"] and "metres" in obj.lower():
            obj = obj.replace("(metres)", "").replace("(metres) ", "").strip()
        if predicate == "floorArea" and "square metres" in obj.lower():
            obj = obj.replace("(square metres)", "").replace("(square metres) ", "").strip()
        return obj

    # Simple phrase templates for each predicate
    # Phrase templates that describe a predicate without repeating the subject.
    # Each template should start with a verb or a relative clause fragment.
    templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": "features {o} architectural style",
        "buildingStartDate": "construction started in {o}",
        "completionDate": "was completed in {o}",
        "floorCount": "has {o} floors",
        "location": "is located at {o}",
        "cost": "costs {o}",
        "floorArea": "has a floor area of {o} square metres",
        "architect": "was designed by {o}",
        "owner": "is owned by {o}",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as {o}",
        "height": "stands {o} metres tall",
        "buildingType": "is a {o}",
        "developer": "was developed by {o}",
        "tenant": "has tenant {o}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {o}",
        "country": "is in {o}",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses {o}",
        "address": "is located at {o}",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on {o}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {o}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {o}",
        "bedCount": "has {o} beds",
        "region": "is in the {o} region",
        "state": "is in the state of {o}",
        "website": "has website {o}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in {o}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number {o}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on {o}",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the significant building {o}",
        "governingBody": "has governing body {o}",
        "leader": "has leader {o}",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor {o}",
        "governmentType": "has government type {o}",
        "capital": "has capital {o}",
        "language": "has language {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {o}",
        "currency": "has currency {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": "includes ethnic group {o}",
        "origin": "originates from {o}",
        "significantProject": "is known for the significant project {o}",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in {o}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {o}",
        "numberOfRooms": "has {o} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "has key person {o}",
        "architecture": "has architecture {o}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {o}",
    }

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            cleaned_obj = clean_object(t.object, t.predicate)
            clause = tmpl.format(s=t.subject, o=cleaned_obj)
        else:
            # Fallback generic phrasing
            clause = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a single sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
